text: The surgeon dissects the root of the left hepatic vein and encircles it with umbilical tape, which is then used to sling the liver. The tape is positioned along the caudate groove and lies along the gallbladder fossa.
The liver sling visually marks the planned transection plane, which follows the middle hepatic vein and divides a segment IV branch of the middle hepatic vein, along with the main left hepatic vein.

The surgeon divides the left hepatic artery and the segment IV hepatic artery at the porta hepatis. The left portal vein and the left hepatic duct are also divided, indicating progression in the liver resection procedure.
